jokes.text: It is a colonoscopy.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Oscar about a mysterious 'procedure' he's having, building concern
jokes.punchline: The reveal that it's just a routine colonoscopy, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven setup with Michael's typical oversharing creating discomfort. The mundane reveal contrasts well with the dramatic buildup.

jokes.text: In your experience, what should I be expecting in terms of sensation... or emotions?
jokes.setup: Michael asking Oscar about a medical procedure
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate intimacy of asking about 'sensations' for a colonoscopy
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael boundary-crossing behavior. The pause before 'emotions' adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Is there anything I can do to make it more pleasurable for me or for Dr. Chaudry?
jokes.setup: Michael continuing to ask inappropriate questions about his colonoscopy
jokes.punchline: Using the word 'pleasurable' about a medical procedure, especially for the doctor
jokes.explanation: Escalates the inappropriate nature of Michael's questions. The inclusion of the doctor makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: My main concern is should I have a safe word?
jokes.setup: Michael treating a medical procedure like something requiring sexual safety protocols
jokes.punchline: The complete inappropriateness of a 'safe word' for a colonoscopy
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdity of the colonoscopy sequence. 'Safe word' is perfectly inappropriate and delivered with Michael's typical earnestness.

jokes.text: Oscar's stunned silence and obvious discomfort
jokes.setup: Michael's escalating inappropriate questions about his colonoscopy
jokes.punchline: Oscar's inability to respond to the safe word question
jokes.explanation: Classic straight-man reaction beat. Oscar's speechlessness sells Michael's absurdity.

jokes.text: I have so much work, I'm gonna blow my brains out.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to get out of a meeting Jim doesn't want him in
jokes.punchline: Casual, dramatic overstatement about having work to do
jokes.explanation: Michael's typical dramatic exaggeration when trying to save face after being excluded.

jokes.text: Because we are the Three Amigos. And once in a while, one of the amigos will go off to the bathroom while the other two have a secret meeting.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's not worried about being excluded from the meeting
jokes.punchline: The absurd analogy comparing a business meeting to going to the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Michael's delusional rationalization. The bathroom metaphor is perfectly inappropriate and revealing.

jokes.text: If you're lying to me right now, your baby is gonna come out a liar. That's how it works. They inherit things through the breast milk.
jokes.setup: Michael interrogating Pam about what she knows about the meeting
jokes.punchline: Completely false science about babies inheriting personality traits through breast milk
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael pseudo-science delivered with total confidence. The breast milk detail makes it more uncomfortable and specific.

jokes.text: Please don't talk about my breast milk.
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate comment about breast milk
jokes.punchline: Pam's perfectly reasonable but uncomfortable request
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response that highlights how inappropriate Michael's comment was.

jokes.text: Really? I'm sorry. I thought this was a free country. I didn't know we were in communist Sweden.
jokes.setup: Darryl telling Dwight to walk away from investigating his injury
jokes.punchline: Dwight's nonsensical invocation of 'communist Sweden' as a response
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight confusion about geography and politics. 'Communist Sweden' is perfectly wrong in the right way.

jokes.text: If we were, I wouldn't have to worry, 'cause we'd have universal health care.
jokes.setup: Dwight complaining about 'communist Sweden'
jokes.punchline: Accidentally making a good point about universal healthcare while missing his own logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment where Dwight accidentally undermines his own point. Shows his confusion while making sharp social commentary.

jokes.text: From who, Dr. J?
jokes.setup: Jim mentioning Darryl has a doctor's note
jokes.punchline: Dwight's skeptical reference to basketball player Julius Erving
jokes.explanation: Quick, economical joke playing on 'Dr. J' nickname. Shows Dwight's suspicious nature.

jokes.text: Y'all having birthday cake? - That's not a very good Darryl.
jokes.setup: Jim doing an impression of Darryl asking about birthday cake
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact critique of Jim's impression
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-humor about impressions within the show. Dwight's blunt criticism is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'll punch you into mush, see?
jokes.setup: Jim talking about being a fan of hardboiled detective novels
jokes.punchline: Terrible impression of a tough detective voice
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Jim's dorky side. The 'see?' makes it appropriately cheesy.

jokes.text: Yes, I do. No, I don't. Yes, I do. No, I don't. Catch you on the flippity-flop.
jokes.setup: Michael being asked to leave the meeting
jokes.punchline: His indecisive response followed by the ridiculous 'flippity-flop' exit line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment of trying to save face while being dismissed. 'Flippity-flop' is absurdly specific.

jokes.text: Had to come in on a Saturday... to retrieve... I left my cell phone here.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he's been working hard, even coming in on Saturday
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic reveal that he only came in to get his phone
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Michael's attempt to seem hardworking. The pause before 'retrieve' builds the anticlimax perfectly.

jokes.text: So jealous of your boobs.
jokes.setup: Kelly talking to pregnant Pam
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate and blunt comment about Pam's pregnancy body
jokes.explanation: Peak Kelly inappropriateness delivered with perfect casualness. Economy is excellent - maximum awkwardness in four words.

jokes.text: Which is why we're having it in Niagara Falls.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining they invited everyone knowing most couldn't make the drive
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of choosing a distant location to avoid having people come
jokes.explanation: Smart writing showing the couple's wedding planning logic. Dry delivery makes the irony work.

jokes.text: That came out wrong.
jokes.setup: Kelly saying she won't go to the wedding if Ryan doesn't because it's 'a waste of time'
jokes.punchline: Her immediate recognition that she just called their wedding a waste of time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kelly's self-awareness. Brief but effective damage control.

jokes.text: I'm in this weird, flirty, nebulous thing with this cousin of mine. And it's a total mind f'er.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why his sales numbers are down
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he's involved with his cousin
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor with Andy's casual delivery of disturbing information. 'Nebulous' is a perfect word choice.

jokes.text: Hey, Andy, let's go visit grandma and then get drunk together.
jokes.setup: Andy reading an email from his cousin
jokes.punchline: The innocent-sounding but contextually inappropriate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the cousin plot. The activities sound normal but become weird in context.

jokes.text: You know when they say it's so crazy it just might work? I don't believe that. I say go for the airtight plan.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his philosophy about plans
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by describing a ridiculous cheese cart plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic failure. Sets up the absurd spy plan with false wisdom about planning.

jokes.text: It is just... elegant.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his plan to hide in a cheese cart
jokes.punchline: Calling this ridiculous plan 'elegant'
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice showing Michael's delusions. The pause before 'elegant' sells his satisfaction with the plan.

jokes.text: From the Wisconsin region, a nice, firm cheddar. Also from the great city of Wisconsin, an aged parmesan.
jokes.setup: Michael doing a fake French accent while presenting cheese
jokes.punchline: Calling Wisconsin a 'great city' and claiming parmesan is from Wisconsin
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael geographic confusion combined with fake sophistication. The cheese expertise is completely wrong.

jokes.text: It's a lot of fun to let the goldfish take a little swim in the blue cheese.
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his fake cheese sommelier act
jokes.punchline: Describing goldfish crackers swimming in blue cheese dressing
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd imagery with Michael's pretentious language applied to kid snacks. 'Little swim' is perfectly childish.

jokes.text: That's a GE. The P40 is much higher pitched.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to identify a train sound to impress David
jokes.punchline: David correcting him with specific technical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's genuine expertise versus Michael's attempted impression. Economical correction.

jokes.text: I'm rebuilding a turn-of-the-century steam engine in my slaughterhouse.
jokes.setup: David talking about his train hobby
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he has a slaughterhouse
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark detail delivered casually. Creates intrigue about David's character depth.

jokes.text: It's just a slaughterhouse, but, sure, any time.
jokes.setup: Michael expressing interest in seeing David's train project
jokes.punchline: David's casual dismissal of having a slaughterhouse as no big deal
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery. 'Just a slaughterhouse' treats something shocking as mundane.

jokes.text: Get the camera. Start taping. Drive! Worker's comp my ass!
jokes.setup: Dwight and Jim conducting surveillance on Darryl
jokes.punchline: Dwight's excited reaction when he thinks he's caught Darryl faking his injury
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Dwight's investigative enthusiasm. 'Worker's comp my ass!' is perfectly Dwight.

jokes.text: You gonna eat all that dog food yourself?
jokes.setup: The confrontation after mistaking Darryl's sister for Darryl
jokes.punchline: Reference to the insult they apparently hurled at her
jokes.explanation: Reveals the absurdity of their mistaken confrontation. Dog food insult is appropriately random and mean.

jokes.text: I once had a glass of cognac that cost $77, so...
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning the wedding costs $75 per person
jokes.punchline: Ryan's pretentious comparison to expensive alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan pretentiousness. The specific price and trailing 'so...' captures his dismissive attitude.

jokes.text: Jim is like... Big Bird. He is tall and yellow and very nice.
jokes.setup: Michael being asked about Jim's management potential
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jim to Big Bird with completely wrong physical characteristics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd analogy that's both wrong and revealing. The 'yellow' detail shows Michael's complete disconnect from reality while trying to sound thoughtful.

jokes.text: Big Bird doesn't make the tough decisions. If I was going to put someone in charge, I would put Bert in charge. Or I would put one of the real grown-ups, like Maria. Or Gordon, maybe.
jokes.setup: Michael's Big Bird analogy for Jim
jokes.punchline: Extending the Sesame Street metaphor to discuss actual management hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation of the absurd analogy. Michael treating Sesame Street characters as real management candidates while making semi-logical points is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Constant office distraction. Spends way too much time at reception. Antagonizes other salesmen. Not at all what he thinks he is.
jokes.setup: Michael reading from Jim's performance review
jokes.punchline: The review is completely accurate from the audience's perspective
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor as the 'negative' review describes exactly what we see Jim do. Michael's dismissal makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'd have to talk to my mother and my guy at H&R block.
jokes.setup: Michael being offered a significant promotion
jokes.punchline: Needing to consult his tax guy and mom about a career decision
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's immaturity and over-reliance on others. H&R Block is a specific, relatable touch.

jokes.text: Example: he handed out jell-o shots at the 23rd mile of the Steamtown Marathon.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he didn't want Michael to help
jokes.punchline: Specific example of Michael's misguided helpfulness during a marathon
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific example that perfectly captures Michael's well-meaning but destructive help. The specificity of '23rd mile' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Did you know that Lincoln's secretary was named Kennedy?
jokes.setup: Tense conversation about the promotion situation
jokes.punchline: Michael's random historical trivia as a deflection
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael deflection technique. The trivia is random enough to derail the conversation.

jokes.text: I should have known that he can do just as much damage in the dark.
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on trying to keep Michael out of the promotion discussion
jokes.punchline: Realizing Michael's destructive power isn't limited by information
jokes.explanation: Good character insight delivered with dry humor. 'In the dark' works both literally and metaphorically.

jokes.text: Why would you think a lady is me?
jokes.setup: Jim and Dwight explaining they mistook his sister for him
jokes.punchline: Darryl's logical confusion about the gender mix-up
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective logic. Darryl's straightforward question highlights the absurdity of their mistake.

jokes.text: Because you look exactly alike.
jokes.setup: Darryl asking why they thought his sister was him
jokes.punchline: Dwight's inappropriate and potentially racist comment
jokes.explanation: Creates appropriate discomfort with Dwight's insensitive comment. Shows his social cluelessness.

jokes.text: Toby Flenderson is doing drugs.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to discredit Jim's performance review
jokes.punchline: Wild accusation against Toby as explanation for the review
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael deflection combining his hatred of Toby with absurd accusation. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm getting pulled over for talking on my cell. Damn it.
jokes.setup: Michael in the middle of sabotaging Jim over the phone
jokes.punchline: Getting interrupted by law enforcement at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony with Michael's sabotage being interrupted by consequences of another bad decision.

jokes.text: Then why is there new wood only on one railing?
jokes.setup: Dwight continuing to investigate Darryl's injury claim
jokes.punchline: Detective-like observation that proves his theory
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Dwight's investigation. Shows his attention to detail and vindicate his suspicions.

jokes.text: I'm telling them you sexually harassed my sister.
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening to file a complaint about Darryl's false claim
jokes.punchline: Darryl's counter-threat with a more serious accusation
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation showing Darryl's leverage. The threat is believable given their earlier encounter.

jokes.text: No judges gonna believe that.
jokes.setup: Darryl's sexual harassment threat
jokes.punchline: Dwight's inappropriately confident dismissal
jokes.explanation: Shows Dwight's social tone-deafness. His confidence about being believed is both funny and concerning.

jokes.text: It would make me feel really good if you would punch me in the face.
jokes.setup: Michael dealing with guilt over sabotaging Jim
jokes.punchline: Asking for physical punishment to relieve his guilt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's guilt and childish approach to making amends. Uncomfortable but genuine.

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Jim saying 'Instead, you screwed me?'
jokes.punchline: Michael's automatic 'That's what she said' response during a serious moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate timing. While predictable, it's perfectly timed during a serious conversation to show his inability to read the room.

jokes.text: Co-manager of what? - Of your butt! And your butt, and your butt... All of Scranton branch butts.
jokes.setup: Stanley asking for clarification about the co-manager title
jokes.punchline: Michael's childish 'butt' response extended to everyone
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael immaturity during what should be a professional announcement. The escalation to everyone's butts is appropriately juvenile.

jokes.text: I'll just have whatever's fanciest, unless there's ribs, I'll get the other information the day of. I'll text you.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to get proper RSVPs for her wedding
jokes.punchline: Creed's complete disregard for wedding planning etiquette
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment showing his complete social cluelessness. The ribs exception and texting plan are perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: You're going to text me the morning of my wedding to ask for directions?
jokes.setup: Creed's casual approach to wedding attendance
jokes.punchline: Pam's incredulous response highlighting how inappropriate his plan is
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman reaction that emphasizes the absurdity of Creed's plan. Her disbelief is justified and relatable.
